name: Highway Inn Kakaako
tagline: Traditional Hawaiian plate lunch since 1947 — the poi, laulau, and pipikaula are as real as it gets.
mustOrder: Laulau — pork and butterfish wrapped in taro and ti leaves, steamed tender
Pipikaula short ribs — Hawaiian-style dried beef, grilled smoky and chewy
Poi — fresh-pounded, the real deal, order it with your plate
parking: Parking lot at SALT at Our Kakaako. Some street parking on Coral St.
talkStory: Founded in 1947 by Seiichi and Nancy Toguchi on the old Waipahu highway connecting sugar plantation camps — hence the name. Now in its fourth generation under Monica Toguchi, who opened the sleek Kakaako location in SALT while keeping the original Waipahu spot alive. The poi is fresh-pounded, the laulau uses traditional taro and ti leaf wrapping, and the pipikaula is smoky perfection. A James Beard semifinalist, Highway Inn bridges old Hawaiian traditions with a modern setting without compromising the recipes.
